findings.search_query: Abijah Ingraham 1755 Connecticut New Brunswick Benjamin Ingraham loyalist Find a Grave
findings.source_title: Abijah Ingraham (c.1755 - c.1832) - Geni
findings.snippet: Abijah Ingraham born May 12, 1755, in Woodbury or Derby, Litchfield, Connecticut, died 1831 in New Brunswick, Canada. Parents Benjamin Ingraham and Mary (Chesebrough). A Loyalist who arrived in Fredericton in fall of 1783; moved his family to the mouth of the Shogomoc River where he built a home and sawmill. He and his brother Benjamin both served in the Kings American Regiment.
findings.claimed_facts: b. 12 May 1755 Litchfield Co., CT; Loyalist (Kings American Regiment), settled New Brunswick 1783 at Shogomoc River; son of Benjamin Ingraham. Worklist mother given as Hannah Tomlinson; this profile says Mary Chesebrough.
findings.notes: Birth date 12 May 1755 and Litchfield Co./Loyalist NB story match worklist. CONFLICT on mother's name: worklist 'Hannah Tomlinson' vs Geni 'Mary Chesebrough'. Death year varies (1831 vs worklist Oct 1832). See also Earle Thomas, 'Greener Pastures: The Loyalist Experience of Benjamin Ingraham' (1983) for the family. Web-tree level confidence.

findings.search_query: Joel Daniel 1759 Alloways Creek New Jersey Sussex Vale New Brunswick loyalist UE
findings.source_title: Joel Daniel UE (1759-1807) | WikiTree
findings.snippet: Birth Oct 19, 1759, Alloway Township, Salem, New Jersey (uncertain). Death 1807, Sussex, Kings, New Brunswick, Canada. Spouse Clarissa (Wynn) McLeod, m. ca 1785, b. 1765 New Jersey. Father William Daniel (1721-1764); Mother Rebecca (Vickery) Daniel (1725-1764). Quaker carpenter disowned by Salem NJ Monthly Meeting 26 Aug 1776 for enlisting; served as Corporal in the New Jersey Volunteers (Loyalist). Sources: Esther Clark Wright, The Loyalists of New Brunswick (1981); Theodore C. Holmes, Loyalists to Canada (1992).
findings.claimed_facts: b. ~19 Oct 1759 Alloway/Salem NJ; d. 1807 Sussex, Kings Co., NB; m. Clarissa Wynn ca 1785; son of William Daniel & Rebecca Vickery; Quaker turned Loyalist (NJ Volunteers).
findings.notes: Strong match to worklist on dates, places, spouse and parents; WikiTree cites multiple published Loyalist sources (Wright 1981; Holmes 1992). Worklist birth 10 Oct vs WikiTree 19 Oct 1759 — minor day discrepancy.

findings.search_query: Mary Taylor Burton New Brunswick 1786 Samuel Bridges Carleton County
findings.source_title: Bridges Family Genealogy (First Generation) - RootsWeb
findings.snippet: Samuel married Mary Robinson Taylor, daughter of James Taylor Esq and Margaret Bartlett, on January 31, 1805. Mary was born May 27, 1786, died January 30, 1862 at age 75, buried in Jacksontown Cemetery, Jacksontown, New Brunswick. Samuel and Mary lost their land in Burton, Sunbury Co. willed by his father and moved to Carleton Co. in 1832.
findings.claimed_facts: b. 27 May 1786; d. 30 Jan 1862, buried Jacksontown Cemetery, NB; m. Samuel Bridges 31 Jan 1805; daughter of James Taylor Esq. & Margaret Bartlett; family moved Burton (Sunbury Co.) to Carleton Co. 1832.
findings.notes: Exact match to worklist birth/death dates, spouse (Samuel Bridges), and parents (James Taylor; Margaret Bartlett). Adds marriage date 31 Jan 1805 and burial at Jacksontown Cemetery. Compiled web genealogy.

findings.search_query: Mary Rowe 1771 Highweek Devon William Reed Kingsteignton Bovey Tracey marriage
findings.source_title: Descendants of William Reed and Mary Rowe - jimlow.com
findings.snippet: William Reed born 1765-06-17 in Kingsteignton, Devonshire; died Bovey Tracey, Devon 1835-07-20; occupation Miller. On 1793-12-22 William married Mary Rowe. The couple had several children.
findings.claimed_facts: Mary Rowe m. William Reed 22 Dec 1793; lived Bovey Tracey, Devon. (Profile centers on husband William Reed of Kingsteignton, miller.)
findings.notes: Confirms Mary Rowe married William Reed (consistent with worklist spouse) and Bovey Tracey/Devon location, plus marriage date 22 Dec 1793. Did not independently verify her 1 Nov 1771 Highweek birth or parents Edward Rowe/Mary Bearne. Personal website, no primary citation.

findings.search_query: Merab Ives 1776 Goshen Connecticut David Ives Greenfield New Brunswick
findings.source_title: Stephen Kinney (1770-1837) | WikiTree
findings.snippet: Merab Ives, daughter of David and Eunice (Gillett) Ives, born 1776 in Connecticut, died May 23, 1843. She married Stephen Kinney, son of Israel and Susannah (Hood) Kinney, b. 1771 Sunbury County, New Brunswick, d. May 3, 1837. They are buried in Greenfield, Wicklow Parish, Carleton County, New Brunswick; married September 18, 1791. David Ives was a Loyalist from Connecticut.
findings.claimed_facts: b. 1776 CT; daughter of David Ives & Eunice Gillett; m. Stephen Kinney 18 Sep 1791; buried Greenfield, Wicklow Parish, Carleton Co., NB. Death given as 23 May 1843.
findings.notes: Match on birth 1776, parents David Ives & Eunice Gillett, and Greenfield NB. NOTABLE: worklist lists NO spouse — source identifies husband as Stephen Kinney (m. 18 Sep 1791). Minor CONFLICT on death: worklist 23 Nov 1842 vs source 23 May 1843. Also see Genealogy of the Ives Family (seekingmyroots G003512).

findings.search_query: William Reed 1765 Kingsteignton Devon Bovey Tracey Mary Rowe Elizabeth Ball
findings.source_title: Descendants of William Reed and Mary Rowe - jimlow.com
findings.snippet: William Reed born 1765-06-17 in Kingsteignton, Devonshire, England. William died in Bovey Tracey, Devon, England, on 1835-07-20; he was 70. His occupation was Miller. On 1793-12-22 when William was 28, he married Mary Rowe.
findings.claimed_facts: b. 17 Jun 1765 Kingsteignton, Devon; d. 20 Jul 1835 Bovey Tracey, Devon; miller; m. Mary Rowe 22 Dec 1793.
findings.notes: Exact match to worklist birth (17 Jun 1765 Kingsteignton), death (20 Jul 1835 Bovey Tracey) and spouse Mary Rowe. Did not confirm parents Elizabeth Ball/William Reed from this source. Personal compiled website.

findings.search_query: Daniel Fairfield 1725 Wenham Massachusetts Vassalboro Maine 1797 Find a Grave
findings.source_title: Fairfield Genealogy / Fairfield Burying Ground (search results)
findings.snippet: Fairfield family records connect Wenham, Massachusetts (Fairfield Burying Ground) and Vassalboro/Maine; WikiTree Fairfield genealogy references a Fairfield from Vassalboro. No single confirmed memorial for Daniel Fairfield (1725-1797) was located in the search.
findings.claimed_facts: Fairfield family associated with Wenham, Essex Co., MA and migration to Maine; no specific record positively matching Daniel Fairfield b. 1725 d. 1797 Vassalboro confirmed.
findings.notes: Only general Fairfield-family/Wenham context found; no individual record positively identified for this Daniel Fairfield. Needs targeted FamilySearch/Vital Records of Wenham lookup. Flagged as weak.

findings.search_query: Abigail Berry 1721 Scarborough Maine George Combs Coombs Brunswick Maine Find a Grave
findings.source_title: Abigail Berry Coombs (1720-1800) - Find a Grave Memorial #39434103
findings.snippet: Abigail Berry Coombs born 1720 in New Meadows, Brunswick, Cumberland, Maine; died March 7, 1800 in the same area; married George Coombs (b. 1717 Gloucester, Essex, MA) ca 1751 in Brunswick. Buried New Meadows Cemetery, Brunswick, Cumberland Co., Maine.
findings.claimed_facts: d. 7 Mar 1800 Brunswick, Cumberland Co., ME; m. George Coombs ca 1751; buried New Meadows Cemetery. Birth shown as 1720.
findings.notes: Find a Grave memorial matches worklist death date 7 Mar 1800, spouse George Combs/Coombs, and Brunswick ME. CONFLICT on birth: FG '1720' vs worklist '31 May 1721'; FG also gives birthplace New Meadows/Brunswick ME, not Scarborough as worklist states. Paired with George Coombs FG #39434101.

findings.search_query: Abner Ripley 1723 Hingham Massachusetts Abigail Robbins Hezekiah Ripley Find a Grave
findings.source_title: Ripley genealogy / Hezekiah Ripley (1695-1779) Find a Grave context
findings.snippet: Abner Ripley b. 1723, sibling of Joshua Ripley, son of Hezekiah Ripley and Sarah Gardiner (married 16 Feb 1716 Hingham, Plymouth Co., MA). Ripley family of Hingham documented.
findings.claimed_facts: b. 1723 Hingham; son of Hezekiah Ripley; part of Hingham Ripley family. Worklist mother 'Sarah Garnet' may correspond to 'Sarah Gardiner' in some trees.
findings.notes: Confirms father Hezekiah Ripley and Hingham origin (matches worklist). Did not locate a specific Abner Ripley memorial or confirm marriage to Abigail Robbins or death 1808. Mother name variant Sarah Garnet vs Gardiner. Needs further lookup.

findings.search_query: Anna Livingston 1736 New Boston New Hampshire Josiah Hitchings St David New Brunswick
findings.source_title: Anna Hitchings (Livingston) (1736 - 1833) - Geni; New Boston to New Brunswick (Acadiensis)
findings.snippet: Anna Livingstone born July 2, 1736 in New Boston, New Hampshire; died at St. David Ridge, St. David Parish, Charlotte County, New Brunswick. Married Josiah Hitchings March 1, 1759 in Chelsea, Massachusetts; daughter of Robert Livingstone and Zebiah Sargent. Hitchings married Ann Livingstone, sister of Deacon Moor's wife Hannah. In autumn 1784 the Cape Ann petitioners were offered land in Wentworth Plantation (became St David Parish); migration from New Boston began the following spring led by Deacon Moor, accompanied by the Hitchings family.
findings.claimed_facts: b. 2 Jul 1736 New Boston, NH; d. 1833 St David Parish, Charlotte Co., NB; m. Josiah Hitchings 1 Mar 1759 Chelsea, MA; daughter of Robert Livingstone & Zebiah Sargent; Loyalist family that settled St David ca 1785.
findings.notes: Strong narrative match to worklist (birth, death place, spouse Josiah Hitchings, Loyalist St David NB). NOTABLE: worklist lists NO parents — sources name parents Robert Livingstone & Zebiah Sargent. Backed by published loyalist history (J.E. Moore, 'Memorial of the loyalist families of William Moore, Josiah Hitchings and Robert Livingstone'; Acadiensis article).

findings.search_query: Annis Pullen Rehoboth Massachusetts John Bragg Nicholas Pullen 1717
findings.source_title: John Bragg (1717-1810) | WikiTree
findings.snippet: Annis Pullen born January 1, 1716/17 in Rehoboth, Bristol, Massachusetts; died before 1800 in Bristol Co., Massachusetts; married John Bragg April 28, 1743 in Rehoboth. Daughter of Nicholas Pullen (and Mary Tucker). Some records render the bride's name as 'Eunice Pullen.'
findings.claimed_facts: b. 1 Jan 1716/17 Rehoboth, Bristol Co., MA; d. bef 1800; m. John Bragg 28 Apr 1743 Rehoboth; daughter of Nicholas Pullen.
findings.notes: Matches worklist birth (1 Jan 1717 Rehoboth), death (bef 1800 Rehoboth/Bristol), spouse John Bragg, father Nicholas Pullen. CONFLICT: some trees record the wife as 'Eunice Pullen' married 28 Apr 1743 — possible name confusion between Annis/Eunice. Adds marriage date and mother Mary Tucker.

findings.search_query: John Pearson 1690 Rowley Massachusetts Newbury 1781 captain Find a Grave
findings.source_title: Capt. John Pearson genealogy (Gapinski Ancestry / WeRelate)
findings.snippet: Capt. John Pearson born 10 Apr 1690 in Rowley; died 26 Feb 1781. Married (1) Elizabeth Mix 24 Mar 1714 in Stonington, New London, CT; (2) Lydia Ilsley. Associated with Rowley/Newbury, Essex Co., MA.
findings.claimed_facts: b. ~Apr 1690 Rowley, Essex Co., MA; d. 26 Feb 1781; Captain; wives Elizabeth Mix and Lydia Ilsley; Rowley/Newbury area.
findings.notes: Death date 26 Feb 1781 and Rowley birth/Newbury area match worklist. CONFLICT on birth day: worklist 6 Apr 1690 vs source 10 Apr 1690. Multiple men named John Pearson in this Rowley family (some b.1685); identity not certain. Worklist gives no spouse/parents to cross-check.

findings.search_query: Cornelius Darling 1732 Bellingham Massachusetts Mehitable Corbett Find a Grave
findings.source_title: Darling family of Bellingham, MA (Find a Grave / Center Cemetery)
findings.snippet: Multiple Darling-family memorials exist in Bellingham, Norfolk Co., MA (Center Cemetery, est. ca 1700). No specific memorial positively matching Cornelius Darling b. 1732 m. Mehitable Corbett was located; nearby trees show Darlings married to a Mehitable (White) in the prior generation.
findings.claimed_facts: Darling family documented in Bellingham, Norfolk Co., MA in this era; no individual record positively confirmed for Cornelius Darling (1732-1816) or his marriage to Mehitable Corbett.
findings.notes: Only general Bellingham Darling-family context; possible confusion with 'Mehitable White' in adjacent generations. No confirmed individual match. Needs targeted FamilySearch/Bellingham vital-records lookup.

findings.search_query: George Combs Coombs 1717 Brunswick Maine Abigail Berry Find a Grave
findings.source_title: George Coombs (1717-1797) - Find a Grave Memorial #39434101
findings.snippet: George Coombs born 1717 in Gloucester, Essex, Massachusetts; died March 2, 1797 in New Meadows, Brunswick, Cumberland, Maine. Married Abigail Berry ca 1751 in Brunswick. In a 1741 map George Coombs owned 300 acres in New Meadows along with his father Peter Coombs (400 acres). Buried New Meadows / Brunswick, ME.
findings.claimed_facts: d. 2 Mar 1797 Brunswick, Cumberland Co., ME; m. Abagail Berry ca 1751; father Peter Coombs; landowner New Meadows 1741. Birthplace given as Gloucester, MA (worklist says New Meadows, Brunswick ME).
findings.notes: Find a Grave matches worklist death 2 Mar 1797 Brunswick ME, spouse Abagail Berry, father Peter Coombs. CONFLICT on birthplace: FG 'Gloucester, Essex, MA' vs worklist 'New Meadows, Brunswick, Maine'. Surname Coombs vs Combs spelling. Paired with Abigail FG #39434103. Also Combs Families of New England (combs-families.org/combs/ms/coombs/04.htm).

findings.search_query: Hannah Norris 1737 Wareham Massachusetts Stephen Washburn Maine
findings.source_title: Stephen Washburn (1736 - 1812) - Geni
findings.snippet: Stephen Washburn b. 24 Sep 1736 Plympton, Plymouth Co., MA, married Hannah Norris on 11 May 1757 in Wareham, Plymouth Co., MA. Hannah Norris b. 21 Aug 1737 in Wareham. Stephen died 1812 in New Gloucester, Cumberland, Maine.
findings.claimed_facts: b. 21 Aug 1737 Wareham, Plymouth Co., MA; m. Stephen Washburn 11 May 1757 Wareham; husband d. 1812 New Gloucester, ME (matches worklist's Maine death context).
findings.notes: Exact match to worklist birth (21 Aug 1737 Wareham) and spouse Stephen Washburn; adds marriage date 11 May 1757. Worklist death 'Jun 1788 Maine' not confirmed by this source. Geni web tree.

findings.search_query: James Guppy 1732 Beverly Massachusetts Ann Loud Dover New Hampshire Joshua Guppy
findings.source_title: Guppy, James, b. 1732 - Portsmouth Athenaeum; Guppy House, Dover NH
findings.source_site: Portsmouth Athenaeum / City of Dover NH
findings.snippet: James Guppy b. 1732 (Portsmouth Athenaeum person record). A James Guppy had a son James (b. 1756 Portsmouth NH) with wife Ann Loud. Capt. James Guppey, a Revolutionary figure, purchased the old homestead (Guppy House) in Dover, NH; family moved to Dover from Portsmouth.
findings.claimed_facts: James Guppy b. 1732, associated with Beverly MA / Portsmouth & Dover NH; wife Ann Loud; family settled Dover, NH. Consistent with worklist (Dover NH death, spouse Ann Loud).
findings.notes: Birth year 1732, spouse Ann Loud, and Dover NH connection match worklist. Some records conflate father James (1732) and son James (1756); Beverly MA birth and parents Joshua Guppy/Margaret Bacon not directly confirmed. Institutional index + town history support existence.

findings.search_query: James Sprague 1746 Harpswell Maine Sarah Dyer Calais Maine Abiel Sprague
findings.source_title: James Sprague (1746-1799) | WikiTree; James Sprague, Sr - Geni
findings.snippet: James Sprague born 1746 in Harpswell, Cumberland, Maine; parents Abiel Sprague (~1710->1773) and Hannah (Getchell) Sprague (1710-1788). Resided Machias and Calais, ME; lumberman and farmer; he and brother Abiel settled in Calais in 1781. Married Jane Dyer (~1753- ). Died 12 Mar 1799 (Scoudouc/Schoodic).
findings.claimed_facts: b. 1746 Harpswell, Cumberland Co., ME; son of Abiel Sprague & Hannah Getchell; settled Calais ME 1781; d. 12 Mar 1799; m. Jane Dyer.
findings.notes: Strong match to worklist birth 1746 Harpswell, death 22 Mar 1799 (WikiTree 12 Mar), father Abiel Sprague, mother Getchell, Calais ME. CONFLICTS: spouse worklist 'Sarah Jane Dyer' vs sources 'Jane Dyer'; death PLACE worklist 'Calais, Washington, Maine' vs sources 'Scoudouc, Westmorland Co., NB' / 'Schoodic, Hancock, ME'. WikiTree + Sprague One-Name Study + 'Sprague Families in America' (archive.org).

findings.search_query: James Taylor Londonderry NH Margaret Bartlett Maugerville New Brunswick loyalist
findings.source_title: TAYLOR, JAMES (1761-1834) - Dictionary of Canadian Biography
findings.snippet: James Taylor born February 1761 in Truro, Nova Scotia, son of Captain Matthew Taylor, a Londonderry Irishman who emigrated to New Hampshire and then Nova Scotia, and Elizabeth Archibald. He married Margaret Bartlett, daughter of Richard Bartlett. Taylor went to the Saint John valley in the 1780s to join the Bartletts who had settled at Burton, Sunbury County; he set himself up as a lumber merchant in the parish of Maugerville near Fredericton.
findings.claimed_facts: Lumber merchant at Maugerville, NB; m. Margaret Bartlett (dau. of Richard Bartlett); d. 27 Jan 1834 Maugerville. Father Capt. Matthew Taylor (Londonderry origin) & Elizabeth Archibald.
findings.notes: Authoritative DCB biography matches spouse Margaret Bartlett, Maugerville NB, and 1834 death. NOTABLE CONFLICT: DCB gives birth Feb 1761 in Truro, Nova Scotia (father a Londonderry Irishman) vs worklist '1754, Londonderry, NH'. The Londonderry link is to the father's origin, not James's birthplace. Also Wikipedia 'James Taylor (1761-1834)'.

findings.search_query: Jemima Carver 1716 Marshfield Massachusetts Jonathan Sylvester Find a Grave
findings.source_title: Family Group Sheet: Jonathan Sylvester / Jemima Carver - WMGS Online Trees
findings.snippet: Jonathan Sylvester and Jemima Carver married about 1738. Jonathan Sylvester born January 28, 1713 (some sources 1714) in Marshfield, Massachusetts; children's births recorded 1740 to early 1760s in Marshfield.
findings.claimed_facts: m. Jonathan Sylvester ca 1738; lived Marshfield, Plymouth Co., MA; children born 1740s-1760s.
findings.notes: Confirms spouse Jonathan Sylvester and Marshfield MA (matches worklist). Did not independently verify her 8 Dec 1716 birth or 1 Mar 1786 death. Husband birth year varies 1713/1714. Compiled society tree.

findings.search_query: Jemima Purdy 1765 Mamaroneck New York John Noble Calais Maine William Purdy
findings.source_title: John Noble (1762-1843) - Find a Grave Memorial #159262517; Jemima Purdy Noble - WikiTree G2G
findings.snippet: Jemima Purdy was a native of Mamaroneck, N.Y., near Washington's encampment. She was captured during the Revolution at Saratoga while aiding the 'enemy' and met John Noble in a British prison in New York, where they were married by a British Chaplain on October 17, 1783. They were one of 14 families living in Calais, Maine in 1790, and had 10 children.
findings.claimed_facts: b. ~1765 Mamaroneck, Westchester Co., NY; m. John Noble 17 Oct 1783 (in a NY British prison); among 14 families in Calais, ME by 1790; 10 children; d. 14 Jan 1859 Calais.
findings.notes: Strong match to worklist: Mamaroneck NY origin, spouse John Noble, Calais ME death. NOTABLE colorful biography (married in a British prison 1783). Birth ~1765 and father William Purdy consistent with Purdy of Rye/Mamaroneck families. John Noble FG memorial + WikiTree discussion thread; corroborated by 'First Families of New Brunswick'.
